title: corydalis pumila
intro: Corydalis pumila, also known as the alpine corydalis or Himalayan corydalis, is a plant species belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to the Himalayas and other mountainous regions. Traditionally, Corydalis pumila has been used in medicine due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and anxiety, as well as promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. Corydalis pumila is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including arthritis, insomnia, and muscle spasms.
